Paths/Backend Development/Authentication & Security

Authentication & Security

Secure your applications with authentication, authorization, and best practices.

Authentication verifies who a user is; authorization determines what they can access.

Key Concepts

  • JWT (JSON Web Tokens): Stateless tokens for authenticating API requests.
  • Sessions: Server-side session management with cookies.
  • OAuth: Third-party login (Google, GitHub) using the OAuth 2.0 protocol.
  • Password Hashing: Never store plain text — use bcrypt or argon2.
  • CORS: Control which domains can access your API.

Example

import jwt from 'jsonwebtoken';
import bcrypt from 'bcrypt';

// Hash password
const hashedPassword = await bcrypt.hash('myPassword', 10);

// Verify password
const isValid = await bcrypt.compare('myPassword', hashedPassword);

// Create JWT
const token = jwt.sign(
  { userId: user.id, role: user.role },
  process.env.JWT_SECRET,
  { expiresIn: '7d' }
);
